title: Boat Hire in Estepona Port: Costs, Boats & 2026 Guide
html:
Boat hire in Estepona Port offers access to the Costa del Sol's finest cruising grounds, with a 6-metre motorboat typically costing from €250 for a half-day. On BoatHire24 you can compare verified listings and prices across dozens of vessels berthed in this lively Andalusian marina.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Estepona Port?

Half-day rentals start around €200 for a small motorboat, while crewed yachts run from €800 for a full day. Prices vary by season, boat size, and whether you hire a skipper. A typical 6-metre open console costs €250, €400 for four hours, and a luxury motor yacht can exceed €1,500 per day. Compare day charters in Estepona Port on BoatHire24 to see specific availability and inclusions. You can day charters in Estepona Port for your chosen dates.

  • Shared sightseeing tour: from €65 per person (2-hour trip)
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): from €250
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): from €600 for up to 10 guests
  • Crewed yacht full-day: from €1,200 including skipper and fuel

Where to set off in Estepona Port

Most charters depart from Puerto Deportivo de Estepona, the main marina at the heart of the port. Other popular departure points include the Real Club Náutico de Estepona and the fishing harbour near the old town. From here you can reach the sandy beaches of Bahía de Estepona, cruise west to the iconic rock of Gibraltar, or head east towards Marbella and Puerto Banús. The calm waters of the Bay of Algeciras are about 12 nautical miles away, offering a sheltered run.

Which boat to rent

The right boat depends on your group size and the kind of day you want on the water. One option worth a look is the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP charter page.

  1. Open console motorboat, ideal for a family day out or coastal exploring; seats 4, 6 guests; from €250 half-day
  2. Rigid inflatable (RIB), fast, nimble, great for watersports; seats 4, 5; from €300 for four hours
  3. Catamaran, stable, spacious, perfect for lounging; seats 8, 12; from €600 full-day
  4. Motor yacht, comfort and style for a luxury cruise; seats 8, 10; from €1,000 full-day (e.g. Azimut or Astondoa models)
  5. Sailboat, for a peaceful day under sail; seats 4, 6; from €350 half-day

For a popular choice among local renters, check out the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open, 150 HP charter page on BoatHire24, which includes specs, photos, and real-time pricing for a 6.05-metre vessel that comfortably carries five passengers.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Estepona Port?

In Spain, boats under 6 metres length and 15 HP can usually be driven without a licence, but many operators require you to hold a valid ICC or equivalent. For larger vessels, a Spanish licence or an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) is mandatory. The local marina operator will confirm the exact requirements for your chosen boat. High season runs from June to September, when the Levante wind can pick up in the afternoons, so morning departures are often best. You can also see more on the BoatHire24 marketplace.

Try the sheltered bay of Playa del Cristo just west of Estepona for a calm anchorage away from the swell, it is rarely crowded and offers crystal-clear water.

At a glance

  • Price range: €200, €1,500 per day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip lengths: half-day (4 hrs) or full-day (8 hrs)
  • Maximum capacity: up to 12 passengers on a catamaran
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Estepona Port

Can I rent a boat for a whole week?

Yes, many owners offer weekly charters, especially for catamarans and motor yachts, with prices from €1,200 per week for smaller boats.

What happens if the weather is bad on my booking day?

Operators will usually offer a reschedule or full refund if the port authority closes the marina due to unsafe conditions.

Is it cheaper to book online or at the marina?

Booking online through a marketplace like BoatHire24 often gives you better prices and cancellation terms than walking up to a kiosk.

Can I bring my own food and drink on board?

Most bareboat charters allow you to bring supplies, but crewed yachts typically include a catering option at an extra cost.

As you plan your 2026 adventure, remember that BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Estepona Port or list your own. Whether you choose a sprightly RIB or a spacious catamaran, the Costa del Sol awaits, from the marinas of Puerto Banús to the quiet coves near Gibraltar. If you own a boat in the region, consider joining the BoatHire24 marketplace to attract guests from across the globe.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Estepona Port and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
